concept: Efficient / High Useful Output Relative to Selected Input
description: Producing a high useful output relative to a selected input. Every efficiency claim carries a hidden boundary and a value choice inside it, since a process can be efficient by one measure while remaining harmful, unjust, or out of reach for the people who need it.
sound_symbolism: 'Po' starts with a brief closure and a rounded release. The stress rolls through 'ru'; energy remains in continuous motion as light 'li' lets the tongue glide into bright 'i'. The whole word spends one closure and carries the rest of its work through liquids, a compact path from effort to result.
grammatical_notes: An adjective placed before a noun or used predicatively: 'poruli terura' (an efficient system); 'ha terura poruli nai.' (This system is efficient.) The quality-noun rule supplies efficiency. State the selected input, useful output, boundary, and conditions when the claim could mislead. Distinct from 'poru' (productive), 'thenoki' (sustain), and 'welao' (good).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Efficiency can reduce resource use, but the word explicitly keeps system boundaries, excluded harms, and chosen benefits available for ethical examination.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The definition resists attachment to a favorable metric by asking what was counted, what was omitted, and under which conditions.

tags.activity: a relation between resources used and useful performance
tags.cognition: an assessment whose result depends on selected inputs, outputs, and boundaries
tags.creation: a comparative quality of designed processes and systems
